text.title: Team AI Usage Policy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Team AI Usage Policy application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Policy lists ALWAYS-OK (public docs, OSS code, synthetic data), NEVER-OK (PII, customer data, secrets, NDA content), ASK-FIRST (internal source code, draft strategy, partial logs).
rule.rationale: Three-bucket model is the only one engineers actually remember.

rule.statement: Policy names exactly which AI tools the team may use. Deny everything not listed. Updates require security approval + vendor-DPA review.
rule.rationale: Shadow IT is the failure mode; explicit allowlist + deny-by-default is enforceable.

rule.statement: Each allowlisted tool's data-retention is documented next to its name. 'Inputs may be used for training' is a flag, not footnote.
rule.rationale: Engineers cannot make informed choices without retention transparency; vendor T&Cs drift.

rule.statement: Policy includes 'I think I leaked' clause: report to security within 2 business hours; do not delete logs; security drives remediation. No retribution for honest self-reports.
rule.rationale: Honest-reporter clauses are the difference between catching incidents and not catching them.

rule.statement: Policy reviewed quarterly by owner. Changes broadcast all-hands; old version archived. Drift >6 months = stale.
rule.rationale: Vendor policies and tool features drift faster than annual cycles; quarterly is the floor.
